business_hours: Starts 8am, days and end time not specified.
emergency_definition: gas station pumps go down (for regular property managers and general contractors)
emergency calls from regular property managers and general contractors
emergency_routing_rules: For regular property managers and general contractors, Ben is on call. Clara will automatically route emergency calls directly to Ben (or whoever is designated). Otherwise, Ben defers emergency calls to other services.
non_emergency_routing_rules: Clara will answer business calls, screen them, take information, take messages, and log details into a dashboard. Notifications (email/text) will be sent. Ben or a designated team member will then review the dashboard and assign the call. Calls from 'preferred contractors' or personal numbers can be configured to bypass Clara and go directly to Ben's phone.
call_transfer_rules: Clara screens calls before transferring. Certain calls (e.g., from Ben's wife, very strong relationship-driven customers) can be configured to bypass screening. If a caller asks for Ben, Clara will ask 'Can you tell me what's it about?' before transferring. Clara will not automatically schedule a job that sends a notification to the customer without final confirmation from Ben.
integration_constraints: Clara is in process of building integration with Jobber. Clara will pull information from Jobber but will not automatically confirm appointments to customers without human approval. Invoicing goes through Jobber (which integrates with QuickBooks).
after_hours_flow_summary: Clara answers calls and takes messages. Clara is programmed to book appointments for the next business day (e.g., after 8 am). For emergencies from regular property managers/general contractors, Ben is on call.
office_hours_flow_summary: Clara answers calls, screens them, takes information, records details in a dashboard, and sends notifications. Ben or a team member reviews the dashboard and assigns tasks. For general calls, Clara can suggest appointment times but Ben makes final confirmation. For direct calls, Clara will screen and transfer to Ben or a designated team member.
questions_or_unknowns: Exact business hours (days and end time, e.g., 'Monday-Friday, 8am-5pm') are not specified, only 'starts 8am'.
Timezone for business hours is unknown.
Last name of operations manager, Greg, is unknown.
Specific rules for which 'certain customers' bypass Clara for direct transfer during office hours need to be defined.
notes: Ben is currently a sole proprietor but plans to incorporate this year. He has 3 full-time journeyman electricians, 1 apprentice (needs to hire another soon), and uses 2 subcontractors, aiming for 5 vans on the road by the end of the year. He uses Jobber for CRM and QuickBooks for accounting/invoicing. He has tried a human virtual assistant in the past, which did not work well. He is bringing on an operations manager/estimator (Greg) to assist with office work and estimating. Ben is currently using his personal cell phone as the business number but plans to get a separate personal number. Clara is being set up on a 3-month trial for $249/month for 500 minutes, paid upfront. The price will be locked in if he renews for a year.
